crypto.c(3) Library Functions Manual crypto.c(3)

NAME

crypto.c - 🔐 Client cryptography: handshake integration, X25519 key exchange, and per-session encryption

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void client_crypto_set_mode (uint8_t mode)
Set crypto mode for handshake (encryption + authentication). int client_crypto_init (void)
Initialize client crypto handshake. int client_crypto_handshake (acip_transport_t *transport)
Perform crypto handshake with server. bool crypto_client_is_ready (void)
Check if crypto handshake is ready. const crypto_context_t * crypto_client_get_context (void)
Get crypto context for encryption/decryption. int crypto_client_encrypt_packet (const uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_len, uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_size, size_t *ciphertext_len)
Encrypt a packet for transmission. int crypto_client_decrypt_packet (const uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_len, uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_size, size_t *plaintext_len)
Decrypt a received packet. void crypto_client_cleanup (void)
Cleanup crypto client resources. bool crypto_client_should_rekey (void)
Check if session rekeying should be triggered. int crypto_client_initiate_rekey (void)
Initiate session rekeying (client-initiated). int crypto_client_process_rekey_request (const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)
Process received REKEY_REQUEST packet from server. int crypto_client_send_rekey_response (void)
Send REKEY_RESPONSE packet to server. int crypto_client_process_rekey_response (const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)
Process received REKEY_RESPONSE packet from server. int crypto_client_send_rekey_complete (void)
Send REKEY_COMPLETE packet to server and commit to new key.

Detailed Description

🔐 Client cryptography: handshake integration, X25519 key exchange, and per-session encryption

CORE RESPONSIBILITIES:

1.
Initialize client crypto context with authentication credentials
2.
Perform cryptographic handshake with server during connection
3.
Manage global crypto context for client connection
4.
Provide encryption/decryption functions for secure packet transmission
5.
Support multiple authentication modes (password, SSH key, passwordless)
6.
Handle session rekeying for long-lived connections

CRYPTOGRAPHIC HANDSHAKE ARCHITECTURE:

The handshake follows a multi-phase protocol:

PHASE 0: PROTOCOL NEGOTIATION:

  • Step 0a: Send client protocol version
  • Step 0b: Receive server protocol version
  • Step 0c: Send client crypto capabilities
  • Step 0d: Receive server crypto parameters

PHASE 1: KEY EXCHANGE:

  • Step 1: Receive server's ephemeral public key and send our public key
  • Client generates ephemeral key pair for this session
  • Both sides derive shared secret using X25519 key exchange

PHASE 2: AUTHENTICATION:

  • Step 2: Receive auth challenge and send response
  • Client signs challenge with identity key (if client has identity key)
  • Server verifies client identity (if whitelist enabled)
  • Step 3: Receive handshake complete message

CRYPTO INITIALIZATION:

The client supports three initialization modes:

1.
SSH KEY MODE (--key specified):
•
Parses Ed25519 private key from file or gpg:keyid format
•
Extracts public key for authentication
•
Supports password-protected keys (SSH agent or prompt)
•
Optional password for dual authentication (key + password)
2.
PASSWORD MODE (--password specified):
•
Uses Argon2id key derivation from shared password
•
Both client and server derive same key from password
•
No identity keys required
3.
PASSWORDLESS MODE (no credentials):
•
Generates random ephemeral keys
•
No long-term identity (no authentication)
•
Suitable for trusted networks or testing

GLOBAL CRYPTO CONTEXT:

The client uses a single global crypto context (g_crypto_ctx):

  • Shared across all connection attempts (reused on reconnection)
  • Initialized once per program execution
  • Cleaned up on program shutdown
  • Stores server connection info for known_hosts verification

SERVER IDENTITY VERIFICATION:

Client verifies server identity using known_hosts:

  • Checks server's identity key against ~/.ascii-chat/known_hosts
  • First connection: Prompts user to accept server key
  • Subsequent connections: Verifies key matches stored value
  • Key mismatch: Warns user about potential MITM attack
  • Optional --server-key for explicit server key verification

CLIENT AUTHENTICATION REQUIREMENTS:

When server requires client authentication (whitelist enabled):

  • Client must provide identity key with --key option
  • Client's public key must be in server's --client-keys list
  • Authentication failure results in connection rejection
  • Interactive prompt warns user if no identity key provided

SESSION REKEYING:

Long-lived connections support periodic rekeying:

  • crypto_client_should_rekey(): Checks if rekey is needed
  • crypto_client_initiate_rekey(): Client-initiated rekeying
  • crypto_client_process_rekey_request(): Handles server-initiated rekey
  • Rekeying refreshes encryption keys without reconnecting

ENCRYPTION/DECRYPTION OPERATIONS:

After handshake completion:

  • crypto_client_encrypt_packet(): Encrypts packets before transmission
  • crypto_client_decrypt_packet(): Decrypts received packets
  • Both functions use global crypto context
  • Automatic passthrough when encryption disabled (--no-encrypt)

ALGORITHM SUPPORT:

The client currently supports:

  • Key Exchange: X25519 (Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman)
  • Cipher: XSalsa20-Poly1305 (Authenticated Encryption)
  • Authentication: Ed25519 (when client has identity key)
  • Key Derivation: Argon2id (for password-based authentication)
  • HMAC: HMAC-SHA256 (for additional integrity protection)

ERROR HANDLING:

Handshake errors are handled appropriately:

  • Server disconnection during handshake: Log and return error
  • Protocol mismatch: Log detailed error and abort connection
  • Authentication failure: Log and return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED
  • Network errors: Detect and handle gracefully (reconnection)
  • Invalid packets: Validate size and format before processing

THREAD SAFETY:

Crypto operations are thread-safe:

  • Global crypto context protected by initialization flag
  • Single crypto context per client process (no concurrent handshakes)
  • Encryption/decryption operations are safe for concurrent use
  • Rekeying operations coordinate with connection thread

INTEGRATION WITH OTHER MODULES:

  • main.c: Calls client_crypto_init() during client startup
  • server.c: Calls client_crypto_handshake() during connection
  • protocol.c: Uses encryption functions for secure packet transmission
  • crypto/handshake.h: Core handshake protocol implementation
  • crypto/keys/keys.h: Key parsing and management functions
  • crypto/known_hosts.h: Server identity verification

WHY THIS MODULAR DESIGN:

Separating cryptographic operations from connection management provides:

  • Clear cryptographic interface for application code
  • Easier handshake protocol evolution
  • Better error isolation and debugging
  • Improved security auditing capabilities
  • Independent testing of crypto functionality

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

Version

2.0

See also

server.c For connection establishment and Crypto Module handshake timing

crypto/handshake.h For handshake protocol implementation

crypto/keys/keys.h For key parsing and management

crypto/known_hosts.h For server identity verification

Definition in file src/client/crypto.c.

Function Documentation

int client_crypto_handshake (acip_transport_t * transport)

Perform crypto handshake with server. Perform crypto handshake with server

Parameters

transport Client's persistent transport (wraps the connected socket)

Returns

0 on success, -1 on failure

Definition at line 435 of file src/client/crypto.c.

435                                                          {
436   // If client has --no-encrypt, skip handshake entirely
437   if (GET_OPTION(no_encrypt)) {
438     log_debug("Client has --no-encrypt, skipping crypto handshake");
439     return 0;
440   }
441 
442   // If we reach here, crypto must be initialized for encryption
443   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_crypto_lc)) {
444     log_error("Crypto not initialized but server requires encryption");
445     log_error("Server requires encrypted connection but client has no encryption configured");
446     log_error("Use --key to specify a client key or --password for password authentication");
447     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED; // No retry - configuration error
448   }
449 
450   log_debug("Starting crypto handshake with server...");
451 
452   START_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
453 
454   // Step 0a: Send protocol version to server
455   protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
456   client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
457   client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
458   client_version.supports_encryption = g_crypto_mode;    // Send crypto mode bitmask
459   client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;             // No compression for now
460   client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
461   client_version.feature_flags = 0;
462 
463   int result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION, &client_version,
464                                           sizeof(client_version), 0);
465   if (result != 0) {
466     log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
467     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
468     return -1;
469   }
470   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Protocol version sent successfully");
471 
472   // Step 0b: Receive server's protocol version
473   packet_type_t packet_type;
474   void *payload = NULL;
475   void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
476   size_t payload_len = 0;
477 
478   result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len, &alloc_buffer);
479   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
480     log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type %u)", packet_type);
481     log_error("Packet type 0x%x (decimal %u) - Expected 0x%x (decimal %d)", packet_type, packet_type,
482               PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION);
483     log_error("This suggests a protocol mismatch or packet corruption");
484     log_error("Raw packet type bytes: %02x %02x %02x %02x", (packet_type >> 0) & 0xFF, (packet_type >> 8) & 0xFF,
485               (packet_type >> 16) & 0xFF, (packet_type >> 24) & 0xFF);
486     if (alloc_buffer) {
487       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
488     }
489     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
490     return -1;
491   }
492 
493   if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
494     log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu, expected %zu", payload_len,
495               sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
496     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
497     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
498     return -1;
499   }
500 
501   protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
502   memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
503   buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
504 
505   // Convert from network byte order
506   uint16_t server_proto_version = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version);
507   uint16_t server_proto_revision = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision);
508   uint8_t server_mode = server_version.supports_encryption;
509 
510   log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (crypto mode: 0x%02x)", server_proto_version, server_proto_revision,
511            server_mode);
512 
513   // Verify server echoed our mode
514   if (server_mode != g_crypto_mode) {
515     log_error("Server mode mismatch: got 0x%02x, expected 0x%02x", server_mode, g_crypto_mode);
516     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
517     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
518   }
519 
520   // Step 0c: Send crypto capabilities to server
521   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Sending crypto capabilities");
522   crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
523   client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(KEX_ALGO_X25519);
524   client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE);
525   client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305 | CIPHER_ALGO_NONE);
526   client_caps.requires_verification = 0; // Client doesn't require server verification (uses known_hosts)
527   client_caps.preferred_kex = KEX_ALGO_X25519;
528   client_caps.preferred_auth = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_AUTH(g_crypto_mode) ? AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 : AUTH_ALGO_NONE;
529   client_caps.preferred_cipher =
530       ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode) ? CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305 : CIPHER_ALGO_NONE;
531 
532   result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CAPABILITIES, &client_caps,
533                                      sizeof(client_caps), 0);
534   if (result != 0) {
535     log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities to server");
536     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
537     return -1;
538   }
539   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Crypto capabilities sent successfully");
540 
541   // Step 0d: Receive server's crypto parameters
542   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Receiving server crypto parameters");
543   payload = NULL;
544   alloc_buffer = NULL;
545   payload_len = 0;
546 
547   result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len, &alloc_buffer);
548   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS) {
549     log_error("Failed to receive server crypto parameters (got type %u)", packet_type);
550     if (alloc_buffer) {
551       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
552     }
553     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
554     return -1;
555   }
556 
557   if (payload_len != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
558     log_error("Invalid crypto parameters packet size: %zu, expected %zu", payload_len,
559               sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
560     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
561     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
562     return -1;
563   }
564 
565   crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params;
566   memcpy(&server_params, payload, sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
567   buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
568 
569   // Convert from network byte order
570   uint16_t kex_pubkey_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.kex_public_key_size);
571   uint16_t auth_pubkey_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.auth_public_key_size);
572   uint16_t signature_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.signature_size);
573   uint16_t shared_secret_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.shared_secret_size);
574 
575   log_debug("Server crypto parameters: KEX=%u, Auth=%u, Cipher=%u (key_size=%u, auth_size=%u, sig_size=%u, "
576             "secret_size=%u, verification=%u)",
577             server_params.selected_kex, server_params.selected_auth, server_params.selected_cipher, kex_pubkey_size,
578             auth_pubkey_size, signature_size, shared_secret_size, server_params.verification_enabled);
579   log_debug("Raw server_params.kex_public_key_size = %u (network byte order)", server_params.kex_public_key_size);
580 
581   // Set the crypto parameters in the handshake context
582   result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&g_crypto_ctx, &server_params);
583   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
584     FATAL(result, "Failed to set crypto parameters");
585   }
586 
587   // Store verification flag - server will verify client identity (whitelist check)
588   // This is independent of whether server provides its own identity
589   if (server_params.verification_enabled) {
590     g_crypto_ctx.server_uses_client_auth = true;
591     g_crypto_ctx.require_client_auth = true;
592     log_info("Server will verify client identity (whitelist enabled)");
593   }
594 
595   // Validate that server chose algorithms we support
596   if (server_params.selected_kex != KEX_ALGO_X25519) {
597     log_error("Server selected unsupported KEX algorithm: %u", server_params.selected_kex);
598     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
599     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
600   }
601 
602   // Validate cipher selection based on negotiated mode
603   bool expect_cipher = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode);
604   if (expect_cipher && server_params.selected_cipher != CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305) {
605     log_error("Server selected unsupported cipher algorithm: %u", server_params.selected_cipher);
606     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
607     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
608   }
609   if (!expect_cipher && server_params.selected_cipher != CIPHER_ALGO_NONE) {
610     log_error("Server chose cipher %u but client requested no encryption", server_params.selected_cipher);
611     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
612     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
613   }
614 
615   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Protocol negotiation completed successfully");
616 
617   // Step 0.5: Send CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO with expected server key (multi-key support)
618   // This allows the server to select the correct identity key from g_server_identity_keys[]
619   if (g_crypto_ctx.verify_server_key && strlen(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key) > 0) {
620     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Sending expected server key to support multi-key selection");
621 
622     // Parse expected server key to get the Ed25519 public key
623     public_key_t expected_keys[MAX_CLIENTS];
624     size_t num_expected_keys = 0;
625 
626     if (parse_public_keys(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key, expected_keys, &num_expected_keys, MAX_CLIENTS) == 0 &&
627         num_expected_keys > 0) {
628       // Send the first expected key (client should only have one expected server key)
629       // Format: 32-byte Ed25519 public key
630       log_debug("Sending CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO with expected server key for multi-key selection");
631       result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO, expected_keys[0].key,
632                                          ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, 0);
633 
634       if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
635         FATAL(result, "Failed to send CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO packet");
636       }
637 
638       // Log the key fingerprint for debugging
639       char hex_key[65];
640       pubkey_to_hex(expected_keys[0].key, hex_key);
641       log_debug("Sent expected server key: %s", hex_key);
642     } else {
643       log_warn("Failed to parse expected server key '%s' - server will use default key",
644                g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key);
645     }
646   }
647 
648   // Step 1: Receive server's public key and send our public key
649   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Starting key exchange");
650   {
651     packet_type_t packet_type;
652     void *payload = NULL;
653     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
654     size_t payload_len = 0;
655     int recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len, &alloc_buffer);
656     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
657       if (alloc_buffer) {
658         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
659       }
660       FATAL(recv_result, "Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT from server");
661     }
662     // Client handshake functions consume the received payload buffer
663     result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(&g_crypto_ctx, transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
664     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
665   }
666   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
667 #ifdef _WIN32
668     // On Windows: Cleanup capture resources before exiting to prevent Media Foundation threads from hanging exit()
669     // Media Foundation creates background COM threads that can block exit() if not properly shut down
670     capture_cleanup();
671 #endif
672     FATAL(result, "Crypto key exchange failed");
673   }
674   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Key exchange completed successfully");
675 
676   // SECURITY: Warn when server requires client verification but client has no identity key
677   bool client_has_identity = (g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519);
678 
679   if (g_crypto_ctx.require_client_auth && !client_has_identity) {
680     // Server requires client verification but client has no identity key
681     log_warn("Server requires client verification but client has no identity key");
682 
683     // Check if we're running interactively (stdin is a terminal)
684     // In debug builds with CLAUDECODE, skip interactive prompts (LLM can't type)
685 #ifndef NDEBUG
686     bool skip_interactive = platform_getenv("CLAUDECODE") != NULL;
687 #else
688     bool skip_interactive = false;
689 #endif
690     if (!skip_interactive && platform_is_interactive()) {
691       // Interactive mode - prompt user for confirmation
692       // Lock terminal for the warning message
693       bool previous_terminal_state = log_lock_terminal();
694 
695       log_plain("\n"
696                 "@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@\n"
697                 "@  WARNING: CLIENT AUTHENTICATION REQUIRED                                    @\n"
698                 "@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@\n"
699                 "\n"
700                 "The server requires client authentication (--client-keys enabled),\n"
701                 "but you have not provided a client identity key with --key.\n"
702                 "\n"
703                 "To connect to this server, you need to:\n"
704                 "  1. Generate an Ed25519 key: ssh-keygen -t ed25519\n"
705                 "  2. Add the public key to the server's --client-keys list\n"
706                 "  3. Connect with: ascii-chat client --key /path/to/private/key\n");
707 
708       // Unlock before prompt (prompt_yes_no handles its own terminal locking)
709       log_unlock_terminal(previous_terminal_state);
710 
711       // Prompt user - default is No since this will likely fail
712       if (!platform_prompt_yes_no("Do you want to continue anyway (this will likely fail)", false)) {
713         log_plain("Connection aborted by user.");
714         exit(0); // User declined - exit cleanly
715       }
716 
717       log_plain("Warning: Continuing without client identity key (connection may fail).\n");
718     } else {
719       // Non-interactive mode (background/script) - just log warning and continue
720       log_warn("Non-interactive mode: Continuing without client identity key (connection may fail)");
721     }
722   }
723 
724   // Step 2: Receive auth challenge and send response
725   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO: Sending auth response to server...");
726   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Starting auth response");
727   {
728     packet_type_t packet_type;
729     void *payload = NULL;
730     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
731     size_t payload_len = 0;
732     int recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len, &alloc_buffer);
733     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
734       if (alloc_buffer) {
735         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
736       }
737       FATAL(recv_result, "Failed to receive auth challenge from server");
738     }
739     // Client handshake functions consume the received payload buffer
740     result = crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(&g_crypto_ctx, transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
741     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
742   }
743   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
744     FATAL(result, "Crypto authentication failed");
745   }
746   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO: Auth response sent successfully");
747   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Auth response completed successfully");
748 
749   // Check if handshake completed during auth response (no authentication needed)
750   if (g_crypto_ctx.state == CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
751     // Propagate encryption flag to crypto context
752     g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx.encrypt_data = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode);
753     // Set crypto context on the persistent transport now that handshake is complete
754     transport->crypto_ctx = (crypto_context_t *)crypto_client_get_context();
755     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(debug, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "client_crypto_handshake",
756                        "Crypto handshake completed successfully (no authentication)");
757     return 0;
758   }
759 
760   // Step 3: Receive handshake complete message
761   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Waiting for handshake complete message");
762   {
763     packet_type_t packet_type;
764     void *payload = NULL;
765     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
766     size_t payload_len = 0;
767     int recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len, &alloc_buffer);
768     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
769       if (alloc_buffer) {
770         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
771       }
772       FATAL(recv_result, "Failed to receive handshake completion from server");
773     }
774     // Client handshake functions consume the received payload buffer
775     result = crypto_handshake_client_complete(&g_crypto_ctx, transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
776     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
777   }
778   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
779     FATAL(result, "Crypto handshake completion failed");
780   }
781 
782   // Propagate encryption flag to crypto context
783   g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx.encrypt_data = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode);
784   // Set crypto context on the persistent transport now that handshake is complete
785   transport->crypto_ctx = (crypto_context_t *)crypto_client_get_context();
786 
787   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(debug, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "client_crypto_handshake", "Crypto handshake completed successfully");
788   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Handshake completed successfully, state=%d", g_crypto_ctx.state);
789   return 0;
790 }




References ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_AUTH,
    ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    AUTH_ALGO_ED25519, AUTH_ALGO_NONE,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::auth_public_key_size,
    buffer_pool_free(), capture_cleanup(),
    CIPHER_ALGO_NONE, CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305,
    protocol_version_packet_t::compression_algorithms,
    protocol_version_packet_t::compression_threshold,
    CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED, crypto_client_get_context(),
    acip_transport::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(),
    crypto_handshake_client_complete(),
    crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(),
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY, crypto_handshake_set_parameters(),
    ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, FATAL,
    protocol_version_packet_t::feature_flags, g_crypto_ctx,
    GET_OPTION, HOST_TO_NET_U16, KEX_ALGO_X25519,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::kex_public_key_size,
    KEY_TYPE_ED25519, lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    log_debug, log_error, log_info,
    log_lock_terminal(), log_plain, log_unlock_terminal(),
    log_warn, MAX_CLIENTS, NET_TO_HOST_U16,
    NS_PER_MS_INT, packet_receive_via_transport(),
    packet_send_via_transport(), PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CAPABILITIES,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
    parse_public_keys(), platform_getenv(),
    platform_is_interactive(), platform_prompt_yes_no(),
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_auth,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_cipher,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_kex,
    protocol_version_packet_t::protocol_revision,
    protocol_version_packet_t::protocol_version, pubkey_to_hex(),
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::requires_verification,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_auth,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_cipher,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_kex,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::shared_secret_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::signature_size, START_TIMER,
    STOP_TIMER, STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_auth_algorithms,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_cipher_algorithms,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_kex_algorithms,
    protocol_version_packet_t::supports_encryption, and
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::verification_enabled.


Referenced by client_main(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), and
  server_connection_establish().






int
  client_crypto_init (void )


Initialize client crypto handshake. Initialize client crypto
    handshake




Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 210 of file
  src/client/crypto.c.


210                              {
211   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Starting crypto initialization");
212 
213   // Reset if already initialized (allows reinit on reconnection)
214   if (lifecycle_reset(&g_crypto_lc)) {
215     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Already initialized, resetting for reinit");
216     crypto_handshake_destroy(&g_crypto_ctx);
217   }
218 
219   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Initializing crypto handshake context");
220   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: crypto_mode=%d (NONE=0, ENCRYPT=1, AUTH=2, FULL=3)", g_crypto_mode);
221 
222   // Check if we have an SSH key, password, or neither
223   int result;
224   bool is_ssh_key = false;
225   private_key_t private_key;
226 
227   // Get string options safely
228   const options_t *opts = options_get();
229   const char *encrypt_key = opts && opts->encrypt_key[0] != '\0' ? opts->encrypt_key : "";
230   const char *password = opts && opts->password[0] != '\0' ? opts->password : "";
231   const char *address = opts && opts->address[0] != '\0' ? opts->address : "localhost";
232   int port = opts ? opts->port : OPT_PORT_INT_DEFAULT;
233   const char *server_key = opts && opts->server_key[0] != '\0' ? opts->server_key : "";
234 
235   // Load client private key if provided via --key
236   if (strlen(encrypt_key) > 0) {
237     // --key supports file-based authentication (SSH keys, GPG keys via gpg:keyid)
238 
239     // For SSH key files (not gpg:keyid format), validate the file exists
240     if (strncmp(encrypt_key, "gpg:", 4) != 0) {
241       if (validate_ssh_key_file(encrypt_key) != 0) {
242         return -1;
243       }
244     }
245 
246     // Parse key (handles SSH files and gpg:keyid format)
247     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Loading private key for authentication: %s", encrypt_key);
248     if (parse_private_key(encrypt_key, &private_key) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
249       log_debug("Successfully parsed SSH private key");
250       log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Parsed key type=%d, KEY_TYPE_ED25519=%d", private_key.type, KEY_TYPE_ED25519);
251       is_ssh_key = true;
252     } else {
253       log_error("Failed to parse SSH key file: %s", encrypt_key);
254       log_error("This may be due to:");
255       log_error("  - Wrong password for encrypted key");
256       log_error("  - Unsupported key type (only Ed25519 is currently supported)");
257       log_error("  - Corrupted key file");
258       log_error("");
259       log_error("Note: RSA and ECDSA keys are not yet supported");
260       log_error("To generate an Ed25519 key: ssh-keygen -t ed25519");
261       return -1;
262     }
263   }
264 
265   if (is_ssh_key) {
266     // Use SSH private key for authentication
267     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Using SSH key for authentication");
268 
269     // Initialize crypto context (generates ephemeral X25519 keys)
270     result = crypto_handshake_init("crypto_client_local", &g_crypto_ctx, false); // false = client
271     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
272       FATAL(result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
273     }
274 
275     // Store the Ed25519 keys for authentication
276     memcpy(&g_crypto_ctx.client_private_key, &private_key, sizeof(private_key_t));
277 
278     // Extract Ed25519 public key from private key
279     g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
280     memcpy(g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.key, private_key.public_key, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
281     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.comment, private_key.key_comment,
282                  sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.comment) - 1);
283 
284     // Extract GPG key ID if this is a GPG key (format: "gpg:KEYID")
285     if (strncmp(encrypt_key, "gpg:", 4) == 0) {
286       const char *key_id = encrypt_key + 4;
287       size_t key_id_len = strlen(key_id);
288       // Accept 8, 16, or 40 character GPG key IDs (short/long/full fingerprint)
289       if (key_id_len == 8 || key_id_len == 16 || key_id_len == 40) {
290         SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id, key_id, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id));
291         log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Extracted client GPG key ID (%zu chars): %s", key_id_len,
292                   g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id);
293       } else {
294         log_warn("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Invalid GPG key ID length: %zu (expected 8, 16, or 40)", key_id_len);
295         g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id[0] = '\0';
296       }
297     } else {
298       // Not a GPG key, clear the field
299       g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id[0] = '\0';
300     }
301 
302     // SSH key is already configured in the handshake context above
303     // No additional setup needed - SSH keys are used only for authentication
304 
305     // Clear the temporary private_key variable (we've already copied it to g_crypto_ctx)
306     sodium_memzero(&private_key, sizeof(private_key));
307 
308     // If password is also provided, derive password key for dual authentication
309     if (strlen(password) > 0) {
310       log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Password also provided, deriving password key");
311       crypto_result_t crypto_result = crypto_derive_password_key(&g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx, password);
312       if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
313         log_error("Failed to derive password key: %s", crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
314         return -1;
315       }
316       g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx.has_password = true;
317       log_debug("Password authentication enabled alongside SSH key");
318     }
319 
320   } else if (strlen(GET_OPTION(password)) > 0) {
321     // Password provided - use password-based initialization
322     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Using password authentication");
323     result = crypto_handshake_init_with_password("crypto_client_local", &g_crypto_ctx, false,
324                                                  GET_OPTION(password)); // false = client
325     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
326       FATAL(result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake with password");
327     }
328   } else {
329     // No password or SSH key - use standard initialization with random keys
330     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Using standard initialization");
331     result = crypto_handshake_init("crypto_client_local", &g_crypto_ctx, false); // false = client
332     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
333       FATAL(result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
334     }
335   }
336 
337   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: crypto_handshake_init succeeded");
338 
339   // Set up server connection info for known_hosts
340   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.server_hostname, address, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.server_hostname) - 1);
341   const char *server_ip = server_connection_get_ip();
342   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: server_connection_get_ip() returned: '%s'", server_ip ? server_ip : "NULL");
343   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.server_ip, server_ip ? server_ip : "", sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.server_ip) - 1);
344 
345   // Use port from server_connection_set_port() if available (e.g., from WebSocket URL),
346   // otherwise use the port from options (TCP/default path)
347   int connection_port = server_connection_get_port();
348   g_crypto_ctx.server_port = (uint16_t)(connection_port > 0 ? connection_port : port);
349   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Set server_ip='%s', server_port=%u", g_crypto_ctx.server_ip, g_crypto_ctx.server_port);
350 
351   // Configure server key verification if specified
352   if (strlen(server_key) > 0) {
353     g_crypto_ctx.verify_server_key = true;
354     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key, server_key, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key) - 1);
355     log_debug("Server key verification enabled: %s", server_key);
356   }
357 
358   // If --require-client-verify is set, perform ACDS session lookup for server identity
359   if (GET_OPTION(require_client_verify) && strlen(GET_OPTION(session_string)) > 0) {
360     log_debug("--require-client-verify enabled: performing ACDS session lookup for '%s'", GET_OPTION(session_string));
361 
362     // Connect to ACDS server (configurable via --acds-server and --acds-port options)
363     acds_client_config_t acds_config;
364     acds_client_config_init_defaults(&acds_config);
365     SAFE_STRNCPY(acds_config.server_address, GET_OPTION(discovery_server), sizeof(acds_config.server_address));
366     acds_config.server_port = GET_OPTION(discovery_port);
367     acds_config.timeout_ms = 5 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;
368 
369     // ACDS key verification (optional in debug builds, only if --discovery-service-key is provided)
370     if (strlen(GET_OPTION(discovery_service_key)) > 0) {
371       log_debug("Verifying ACDS server key for %s...", acds_config.server_address);
372       uint8_t acds_pubkey[32];
373       asciichat_error_t verify_result =
374           discovery_keys_verify(acds_config.server_address, GET_OPTION(discovery_service_key), acds_pubkey);
375       if (verify_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
376         log_error("ACDS key verification failed for %s", acds_config.server_address);
377         return -1;
378       }
379       log_debug("ACDS server key verified successfully");
380     }
381 #ifndef NDEBUG
382     else {
383       log_debug("Skipping ACDS key verification (debug build, no --discovery-service-key provided)");
384     }
385 #endif
386 
387     acds_client_t acds_client;
388     asciichat_error_t acds_result = acds_client_connect(&acds_client, &acds_config);
389     if (acds_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
390       log_error("Failed to connect to ACDS server at %s:%d", acds_config.server_address, acds_config.server_port);
391       return -1;
392     }
393 
394     // Perform SESSION_LOOKUP to get server's identity
395     acds_session_lookup_result_t lookup_result;
396     acds_result = acds_session_lookup(&acds_client, GET_OPTION(session_string), &lookup_result);
397     acds_client_disconnect(&acds_client);
398 
399     if (acds_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || !lookup_result.found) {
400       log_error("ACDS session lookup failed for '%s': %s", GET_OPTION(session_string),
401                 lookup_result.found ? "session not found" : "lookup error");
402       return -1;
403     }
404 
405     // Convert server's Ed25519 public key (32 bytes) to hex string
406     char server_key_hex[65]; // 32 bytes * 2 + null terminator
407     for (size_t i = 0; i < 32; i++) {
408       SAFE_SNPRINTF(&server_key_hex[i * 2], 3, "%02x", lookup_result.host_pubkey[i]);
409     }
410     server_key_hex[64] = '\0';
411 
412     // Set expected server key for verification during handshake
413     g_crypto_ctx.verify_server_key = true;
414     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key, server_key_hex, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key) - 1);
415     log_debug("ACDS session lookup succeeded - server identity will be verified");
416     log_debug("Expected server key (from ACDS): %s", server_key_hex);
417   }
418 
419   // Mark initialization as complete
420   lifecycle_init(&g_crypto_lc, "client_crypto");
421 
422   log_debug("Client crypto handshake initialized");
423   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Initialization complete");
424   return 0;
425 }






References acds_client_config_init_defaults(),
    acds_client_connect(), acds_client_disconnect(),
    acds_session_lookup(), options_state::address,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, crypto_derive_password_key(),
    crypto_handshake_destroy(), crypto_handshake_init(),
    crypto_handshake_init_with_password(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_result_to_string(), discovery_keys_verify(),
    ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, options_state::encrypt_key,
    FATAL, acds_session_lookup_result_t::found,
    g_crypto_ctx, GET_OPTION,
    acds_session_lookup_result_t::host_pubkey,
    private_key_t::key_comment, KEY_TYPE_ED25519,
    lifecycle_init(), lifecycle_reset(), log_debug,
    log_error, log_warn, MS_PER_SEC_INT,
    OPT_PORT_INT_DEFAULT, options_get(),
    parse_private_key(), options_state::password,
    options_state::port, private_key_t::public_key,
    SAFE_SNPRINTF, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    acds_client_config_t::server_address,

896                                        {
897   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_crypto_lc) || !crypto_client_is_ready()) {
898     log_error("Cannot initiate rekey: crypto not initialized or not ready");
899     return -1;
900   }
901 
902   acip_transport_t *transport = server_connection_get_transport();
903   if (!transport) {
904     log_error("Cannot initiate rekey: invalid transport");
905     return -1;
906   }
907 
908   asciichat_error_t result = crypto_handshake_rekey_request(&g_crypto_ctx, transport);
909   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
910     log_error("Failed to send REKEY_REQUEST: %d", result);
911     return -1;
912   }
913 
914   return 0;
915 }

948                                             {
949   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_crypto_lc) || !crypto_client_is_ready()) {
950     log_error("Cannot send rekey response: crypto not initialized or not ready");
951     return -1;
952   }
953 
954   acip_transport_t *transport = server_connection_get_transport();
955   if (!transport) {
956     log_error("Cannot send rekey response: invalid transport");
957     return -1;
958   }
959 
960   asciichat_error_t result = crypto_handshake_rekey_response(&g_crypto_ctx, transport);
961   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
962     log_error("Failed to send REKEY_RESPONSE: %d", result);
963     return -1;
964   }
965 
966   return 0;
967 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, crypto_client_is_ready(),
    crypto_handshake_rekey_response(), g_crypto_ctx,
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), log_error, and
    server_connection_get_transport().






bool crypto_client_should_rekey (void )


Check if session rekeying should be triggered. Check if session
    rekeying should be triggered




Returns


true if rekey should be initiated, false otherwise


Definition at line 882 of file
  src/client/crypto.c.


882                                       {
883   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_crypto_lc) || !crypto_client_is_ready()) {
884     return false;
885   }
886   return crypto_handshake_should_rekey(&g_crypto_ctx);
887 }






References crypto_client_is_ready(),
    crypto_handshake_should_rekey(), g_crypto_ctx, and
    lifecycle_is_initialized().
